data: Text is set out over six lines within a panel of 26 x 21 cm in the form of a tabula ansata

Lunate omega, sigma and epsilon; alpha, lamda and delta have extended upper strokes; mu appears in two forms on line 2, typical later form with tall upper strokes and at line end a small rounded form mid-line; xsi on line 4 is formed of two horizontals with a detached curved stroke between. The repetition of the deceased's name on line 3 is marked off by a large comma-shaped interpunct.
later 3rd century CE (Ferrua on letter forms)
Contrada Reina, close to the railway line to Noto, found 13.05.1930
Photo J.Prag: Aut. Assessorato Beni Culturali Regione Siciliana n.10681 del 06/05/2014

An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
Antonio Ferrua, «Epigrafica sicula pagana e cristiana», Rivista di Archeologia Cristiana 18 (1941): 151–243
Noto greca e romana:fonti storiografiche,epigrafi e pseudo-monete

Giacomo Manganaro, «Noto greca e romana:fonti storiografiche,epigrafi e pseudo-monete», in Contributi alla geografia storica dell’agro netino. Atti delle Giornate di Studio. Noto, Palazzo Trigona, 29-31 maggio 1998, a c. di F. Balsamo e V. La Rosa (Rosolini, 2001), 73–96
Robert Crellin integrated lemmatizations into EpiDoc
Ferrua, 1941, Epigrafica sicula pagana e cristiana
Manganaro, 2001, Noto greca e romana:fonti storiografiche,epigrafi e pseudo-monete

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text from autopsy

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Thalpon, farewell, worthy and blameless Thalpon! Here I lie, having died aged six, a child of swift fate.

Italian translation

Talpone, addio, buono ed irreprensibile Talpone! Qui giaccio, e morto a sei anni, bambine di celere fato.

commentary

The name Thalpon is not otherwise attested.

bibliography

  • Ferrua 1941 217-218 no.99 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001456
  • Manganaro 2001 87 no.VII bis https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001806

The repetition of the deceased's name on line 3 is marked off by a large comma-shaped interpunct.

Text from autopsy

Thalpon, farewell, worthy and blameless Thalpon! Here I lie, having died aged six, a child of swift fate.

Talpone, addio, buono ed irreprensibile Talpone! Qui giaccio, e morto a sei anni, bambine di celere fato.

The name Thalpon is not otherwise attested.
